items.title: Configure Dependabot to keep dependencies fresh
items.rationale: No automated dependency scanner detected — vulnerable transitive deps go unflagged.
items.fix_hint: Drop a `.github/dependabot.yml` that updates GitHub Actions and your language ecosystems weekly.
items.suggested_patch:
--- a/.github/dependabot.yml
+++ b/.github/dependabot.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+version: 2
+updates:
+  - package-ecosystem: "github-actions"
+    directory: "/"
+    schedule:
+      interval: "weekly"
+  - package-ecosystem: "pip"
+    directory: "/"
+    schedule:
+      interval: "weekly"

items.title: None of these expected paths exist:
items.rationale: None of these expected paths exist: .gitleaks.toml, .gitleaks.yaml, .gitleaks.yml, gitleaks.toml, .trufflehog.yaml, .trufflehog.yml, .secrets.baseline, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml, .github/workflows/secret-scan.yml
items.fix_hint:
Add a `.gitleaks.toml` (or `.secrets.baseline` for detect-secrets) and
wire it into pre-commit or a GitHub Actions workflow. Example:
https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks-action.

items.title: At least one ecosystem-specific lockfile is committed
items.rationale: At least one ecosystem-specific lockfile is committed. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Run `poetry lock` / `npm install` / `cargo update` (whichever your
manifest needs) and commit the resulting lockfile. For pip-only
Python projects, freezing `requirements.txt` to exact versions or
switching to `uv` / `poetry` is acceptable.

items.title: No project manifest found (pyproject
items.rationale: No project manifest found (pyproject.toml, package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, ...).
items.fix_hint:
Drop a `pyproject.toml` (or `package.json` / `Cargo.toml` / etc.)
at the repo root with at least name + version + dependencies. For
monorepos, every package directory should have its own manifest.

items.title: Large file: fixtures/noisy/src/noisy_fixture/generated
items.fix_hint:
Split the file into smaller modules grouped by concept, or add an
exclusion in `.agent-readiness.toml` if the file is truly intentional
(large generated tables, etc.).

items.title: None of these expected paths exist:
items.rationale: None of these expected paths exist: .coveragerc, codecov.yml, .codecov.yml, coveralls.yml, .coveralls.yml
items.fix_hint:
Add a coverage config (`.coveragerc` for pytest-cov, `codecov.yml` for
Codecov) so coverage signal is visible to both humans and agents.
